title: [cross-file] lichen_link_cleanup() called without corresponding lichen_replay_table_init()
description:
Interaction: lichen/link_ctx.h:lichen_link_cleanup <-> lichen_l2.c:replay_table

Bug: lichen_link_cleanup() (link_ctx.h:148-156) clears ed25519_sk, link_key, and resets has_key/has_link_key/tx_seq. But it doesn't clear or notify the replay table.

In lichen_l2.c, cleanup is called in multiple places:
1. fail_late_init (line 1559): lichen_link_cleanup() called, but replay_table not explicitly cleared (though it may be zero from init)
2. lichen_l2_enable disable path (line 1147): lichen_link_cleanup() called, then lichen_replay_table_init() at line 1157 - CORRECT
3. lichen_l2_iface_init re-init path (line 1430): cleanup called, but replay_table_init at line 1440 - CORRECT

Actually, checking the re-init path more carefully:
- Line 1416 checks if link_ctx_initialized
- Lines 1427-1430: acquire mutexes, clear link_ctx_initialized, clear peer_table, cleanup link_ctx
- Line 1439-1440: init link_ctx, init replay_table

The replay_table_init happens AFTER the mutexes are released (line 1431-1432). But wait, in the re-init block the mutexes ARE held (1427-1430). Then 1431-1432 releases. Then 1439-1440 does the init without holding mutexes.

This is a race: between lines 1432 and 1440, another thread could access the replay_table (via RX callback that somehow got registered). But at this point link_ctx_initialized is 0, so lichen_l2_input would bail early at line 1664.

Actually the code is safe. Not a bug.
close_reason: Not a bug. All cleanup paths that call lichen_link_cleanup() either: (1) also call lichen_replay_table_init() (enable disable path, iface_init re-init path), or (2) are protected by link_ctx_initialized=0 which prevents RX from accessing stale replay_table.
